topic: Part 6.13 MOND grouping — c6 verification of c5 H1 (critical) kernel rebuild around WIN-054 title + c5 H2 forward-pointer patches (queue_id 390)
change_summary: Decider applied major kernel-h3 rewrite removing false ΛCDM Counter-Prediction attribution. Kernel now anchors on WIN-054 actual published title (El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility). c5 H2 forward-pointer phrasing also patched. WIN-048 detail_evidence corrected upstream (Counter-Prediction phrase fully removed from both files).
our_argument_summary: The MOND-grouping subsection argues that five late-March WINs (WIN-047/048/052/054/055) import the canonical MOND-community anomaly list without any disc-cosmology derivation. The dome model cannot reach galactic-scale physics from its bounded firmament (disc_radius ≈ 20,015 km, firmament_height ≈ 9,086 km, all phenomena within ~10⁷ m), so derivation is structurally foreclosed. The placement-as-confirmed-WIN is the self-own: confirmation requires the dome to contain the phenomenon, which it cannot. Verdict: Self-Contradicted (sub-rationale: category-error import / MOND grouping). The kernel-of-truth now concedes the dome author had the rhetorical category available (WIN-054 alone uses anti-ΛCDM Impossibility framing in its title) and used it in exactly one of the five MOND-grouping WINs.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py automation for these grouping WINs. The dome relies on published anomaly claims from MOND-community papers (Asencio+2023, Mistele 2024, Banik & Zhao 2022). No ECM-derived numerical predictions exist for any of the five WINs.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: None of the five WIN values are computed from dome geometry. All are imported from external literature.
data_sources_used: no live API fetches for WIN-047/048/052/054/055
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Each WIN uses textually-identical 'consistent with' / 'compatible with' / 'ECM accommodates' language — no committed numerical prediction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — the section makes no specific dome-code claims about these WINs beyond the absence of derivation, which is correct.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain or data fetch for WIN-047/048/052/054/055. The grouping argument operates at the section level, not per-WIN automation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: These WINs do not relabel a standard-physics explanation — they import an alternative-cosmology critique (MOND-community anomaly list) without derivation. Distinct from the relabel pattern.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: All five anomalies were published in the MOND-community literature (Asencio et al. 2023, Mistele 2024, McGaugh+2016, Banik & Zhao 2022) before the dome added them as WINs on 2026-03-23. Same-day registration + same-day 'confirmation' confirms post-hoc adoption.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section establishes (h3#3) that derivation is structurally foreclosed by the dome bounded-firmament commitment (max spatial extent ~10⁷ m vs MOND-relevant scales ~10²⁰–10²⁵ m). No dome-side derivation chain exists or could exist.

holes_found.description: Kernel-self-own WIN-054 title-quote redundancy (regression of ISS-2135 pattern). The c5 H1 critical fix correctly removed the false ΛCDM Counter-Prediction attribution and rebuilt the kernel around WIN-054 actual title (Option A as recommended). However, the identical title-quote intro now appears verbatim in TWO h3 blocks: (a) kernel h3 paragraph 1: 'WIN-054 published title on the dome wins page is El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility — the word Impossibility frames the claim as a ΛCDM failure, and the dome own prose confirms it: El Gordo is the strongest single-object challenge to ΛCDM in existence.' (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping kernel h3); (b) self-own h3 paragraph 2: 'WIN-054 published title on the dome wins page is El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility — that phrasing names the argument form directly, and the dome own prose confirms it: El Gordo is the strongest single-object challenge to ΛCDM in existence.' (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3). Same subject, same predicate, same quoted dome title, same quoted dome prose; only the explanatory clause varies ('frames the claim as ΛCDM failure' vs 'phrasing names the argument form directly' — these are paraphrases of each other). This is structurally identical to ISS-2135 (c3 H1 kernel-self-own redundancy on WIN-048) which was closed via EXP-428 — the c5 H1 fix migrated the same redundancy pattern to WIN-054. The c5 H2 (moderate) review explicitly anticipated this risk: 'Resolved as a side-effect of H1 fix if Option A is taken — restructuring the kernel to use WIN-054 title naturally pushes the WIN-048-as-counter-prediction-style content into the self-own h3 (where it currently lives in paragraph 2) without kernel duplication.' That natural compression did not happen during the c5 patch; both paragraphs retain the full title-quote intro.
holes_found.recommendation: Compress the self-own h3 paragraph 2 opening to reference back to the kernel rather than re-quoting WIN-054 title-and-prose. Suggested rewrite: 'The dome author does signal in some WIN titles when the argument is the standard model has trouble with this rather than my model predicts X — WIN-054 Impossibility framing (kernel above) is the case-in-point. The dome also publishes a formal claim taxonomy on audit.html that distinguishes Retrospective structural arguments...' This keeps the self-own h3 paragraph 3 punchline (Filing WIN-054 as confirmed WIN... 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility title is the exception that exposes the pattern) intact — readers reach that punchline without the verbatim title-quote being repeated.

holes_found.description: c4 H2 / c5 H3 parenthetical verbosity carry-over (not blocking, c5 marked not-blocking). Section h3#3 paragraph 5 closing parenthetical: '...the dome has produced no derivation from firmament-projection mechanics that would substitute for it on any of these specific signatures (see WIN-052 detail above and the no-derivation-chain argument earlier in this subsection).' The c4 H2 dedup fix correctly removed the duplicate WIN-052 anchor but replaced it with this dual-pointer phrase. Three nested clauses by end of a 12+ line sentence. The forward-reference adds reading load without new information (the no-derivation-chain argument is two paragraphs prior and the reader has already absorbed it). c5 H3 explicitly marked this not-blocking, so it is carry-forward minor.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional minor edit — simplify closing parenthetical to '(see WIN-052 detail above)' or drop entirely. Not blocking; current version is correct, just verbose.

holes_found.description: ISS-2137 (c3 H4, status assigned-analyst) is de facto resolved by the c5 kernel rewrite — but the issue is still open in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json. ISS-2137 description: 'Kernel h3 says ΛCDM sub-label would have produced a defensible — if much weaker — claim structure. Later closed-world h3 argues no framing rescues these items because bounded firmament cannot accommodate galactic-scale physics.' The c5 kernel REMOVED the 'defensible — if much weaker — claim structure' language entirely. Current kernel says only 'The author had the right concept and used it in at least one title' — no claim of defensibility-under-different-framing. The tension ISS-2137 flagged no longer exists in the deployed prose. Decider housekeeping issue, not a section content issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider can close ISS-2137 as 'resolved by c5 kernel rebuild (EXP-428 follow-up / queue_id 389 patch)' — current kernel no longer makes the defensibility concession that produced the tension. No further section edit required for this point.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c5 kernel rewrite is a genuine improvement: it replaced a factually-wrong dome-attribution (the fabricated ΛCDM Counter-Prediction sub-label that did not exist on https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html or audit.html, fetched 2026-05-19 and 2026-05-20) with a verifiable dome quote (WIN-054 published title El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility, verified verbatim at line 619 of the fetched dome wins.html on 2026-05-20). The kernel concession is honestly calibrated: among WIN-047/048/052/054/055, only WIN-054 uses anti-ΛCDM framing in its title (verified by inspecting all five title strings in the dome wins.html fetched 2026-05-20). The other four (WIN-047 Low-z Hubble Law from Aetheric Redshift, WIN-048 CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction, WIN-052 RAR Lensing Extension (Mistele 2024), WIN-055 Independent Distance-Redshift Confirmation (Cepheid/SBF)) all use pro-ECM or neutral framing. The kernel one-of-five framing is accurate (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping kernel h3).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel improvement does not save the placement-as-WIN argument because the closed-world h3 (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping h3#3) operates independently: even if all five MOND-grouping WINs were re-titled with WIN-054 Impossibility framing, the dome bounded firmament (disc_radius ≈ 20,015 km, firmament_height ≈ 9,086 km, max spatial extent ~10⁷ m) still cannot host the galactic-scale 3D physics (10²⁰–10²⁵ m scales) where MOND-relevant phenomena live. The kernel concession credits the rhetorical category-recognition; the self-own h3 argues the placement-as-WIN remains incoherent regardless of framing, because confirmation requires the dome model to contain the phenomenon and produce the value, and bounded firmament forecloses that. All five WIN verdicts (data/wins.json:WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055 → Self-Contradicted) survive intact (verified 2026-05-20).

deeper_analysis:
This is a verification review of the c5 critical fix. The c5 review flagged a factually-wrong dome-attribution as load-bearing on the kernel-of-truth concession, and recommended Option A (rebuild kernel around WIN-054 actual title). The decider patch implements Option A cleanly: ΛCDM Counter-Prediction is gone from both data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence (0 occurrences, verified) AND sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping (0 occurrences, verified). The new kernel anchor is verifiable verbatim against the dome published surface — direct fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html on 2026-05-20 returns line 619 'WIN-054 — El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility CONFIRMED 2026-03-23' and line 624 'El Gordo is the strongest single-object challenge to ΛCDM in existence.' Both quotes match the kernel verbatim. The dome may move/retitle this WIN in future, but as of this review the citation is bulletproof.

The queue reason asked specifically: 'Verify kernel argument now uses verifiable dome quote; self-own paras 2+3 coherent.' Verifiable dome quote: VERIFIED. Self-own paras 2+3 coherence: para 2 establishes the dome HAS the rhetorical category available, para 3 argues that despite having it the placement-as-WIN is the self-own (the dome could have filed these as ΛCDM-trouble entries but chose the strongest claim category). Para 2 + para 3 are logically coherent — but para 2 is now redundant with the kernel (the new moderate hole). The redundancy does not break coherence; it just makes the prose verbose at the structural seam where kernel-of-truth hands off to self-own.

Verdict-holds verification: all five MOND-grouping WINs (047/048/052/054/055) carry verdict=Self-Contradicted in data/wins.json (verified 2026-05-20). Section sub-rationale text matches. ISS-2137 (c3 H4 carry-over) is now de facto resolved by the c5 kernel rewrite — the defensibility-under-different-framing concession that ISS-2137 cited is no longer in the prose. Recommend decider close ISS-2137 as housekeeping.

The critical fix lands cleanly. The recommended minor_edit is compression of the self-own h3 paragraph 2 opening (drop the WIN-054 title-quote re-introduction, reference the kernel instead). The argument structure, citations, verdict, and load-bearing claims all hold.

Thinking about the other side: a dome defender could argue that the kernel-self-own redundancy is intentional bracketing — opening with the WIN-054 concession in the kernel and closing the self-own with it for rhetorical symmetry. That defense is not unreasonable; many academic-style arguments use thesis-and-recapitulation. But our concession is honest only if the section is also tight; verbosity at the structural seam dilutes the punch. The defender critique is real but minor — recommending compression rather than restructure.

citations_verified: WIN-054 dome title El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility — verified verbatim against fetched dome wins.html line 619 (2026-05-20)
WIN-054 dome prose El Gordo is the strongest single-object challenge to ΛCDM in existence — verified verbatim against fetched dome wins.html line 624 (2026-05-20)
WIN-048 dome title CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction — verified against fetched dome wins.html line 550 (2026-05-20)

Five WIN titles for the MOND grouping all checked against dome wins.html (2026-05-20): WIN-047 Low-z Hubble Law from Aetheric Redshift; WIN-048 CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction; WIN-052 RAR Lensing Extension (Mistele 2024); WIN-054 El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility; WIN-055 Independent Distance-Redshift Confirmation (Cepheid/SBF). Only WIN-054 carries anti-ΛCDM framing in its title (1/5 = the kernel one-of-five framing is accurate).

Counter-Prediction phrase: 0 occurrences in current data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence (was the false attribution); 0 occurrences in current data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping (c5 H1 critical fix verified clean across both files)
All five MOND-grouping WIN verdicts: data/wins.json:WIN-047/048/052/054/055 all = Self-Contradicted (verified 2026-05-20)

summary_for_decider: c5 H1 critical fix is VERIFIED CLEAN. Counter-Prediction phrase is 0/0 across both data/wins.json and sections.json. Kernel rebuild on WIN-054 anchor is verified verbatim against dome wins.html (fetched 2026-05-20). Section verdict (Self-Contradicted for all 5 WINs) holds. Three findings: (1) NEW moderate — kernel-self-own WIN-054 title-quote redundancy (regression of ISS-2135 pattern, migrated from WIN-048 to WIN-054 during c5 patch; c5 H2 anticipated this risk and it materialized). Recommend compressing self-own h3 paragraph 2 opening to reference kernel. (2) Minor — c4 H2 / c5 H3 parenthetical verbosity carry-over (not blocking per c5). (3) Minor housekeeping — ISS-2137 (c3 H4 carry-over, status assigned-analyst) is de facto resolved by c5 kernel rewrite; the defensibility-under-different-framing language ISS-2137 flagged is no longer in the prose. Decider can close ISS-2137 with reason 'resolved by c5 kernel rebuild'. Recommended action: minor_edit on the moderate; the section is otherwise in good shape post-c5.
